_note: Round-15 follow-up #4: destructure-default handling now mirrors upstream's VALUE-level IIFE shape (`slot.ts:117`): `((PATTERN) => leaf)(undefined as any as ELEMENT_TYPE)`. The previous type-level approximation (`Exclude<T, undefined> | <typeof default>`) lost fidelity for object / array / template-literal defaults. The hardest break: a template-literal default with a value-level interpolation (`\`${PREFIX}fallback\``) was passed through into a TS TYPE position by `default_typeof_expr` — TS template literal types require type-level interpolations, so a value identifier inside `${…}` produced TS2304 on the emitted overlay. Post-fix the destructure pattern is placed at VALUE level inside the IIFE; TS evaluates the default normally and `PREFIX` resolves through closure. Producer here uses `{ value = \`${PREFIX}fallback\` }` over `Row[]` whose `value` is `undefined`. Post-fix `value`'s type is `string` (the template-literal default kicks in); Consumer's `takesString(v)` passes clean. Pre-fix the overlay emitted `\`${PREFIX}fallback\`` as a TS type and tsgo fired TS2304 on the synthesized overlay before the consumer's check could run.
